# Regime scoring comparison (issue #2)

Backtest period: **1980-01-01 -> 2024-12-31 (45.0y)**. Rebalance: quarterly. Costs: default schedule.

## Results

| Strategy | CAGR | Vol | Sharpe | Max DD | Calmar | Avg Turnover |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| AllWeather | 8.28% | 8.59% | 0.96 | -22.9% | 0.36 | 0.030 |
| BigCycle binary | 8.09% | 9.72% | 0.83 | -32.4% | 0.25 | 0.055 |
| BigCycle scored | 7.84% | 9.35% | 0.84 | -31.3% | 0.25 | 0.064 |

## Interpretation

Over 1980-01-01 -> 2024-12-31 (45.0y), the score-blended BigCycle variant posted a Sharpe of 0.84 vs 0.83 for the binary version and 0.96 for AllWeather. The continuous scoring path is statistically indistinguishable from the binary classifier on Sharpe (delta +0.01); CAGR delta -0.25%, max-drawdown delta +1.19% (positive means shallower drawdown).

More striking than the binary-vs-scored wash: both BigCycle variants trail AllWeather on max drawdown (-32.4% binary, -31.3% scored vs -22.9% for AllWeather; gaps of -9.5% and -8.3% respectively). On this period that points at the base allocations as the more actionable lever than further classifier refinement. Caveat: 1980-2024 is a regime that broadly favored bond-heavy allocations like AllWeather, so its lead here should be read narrowly and not extrapolated to regimes the backtest doesn't cover.

## Method

- `AllWeather`: Bridgewater-style fixed mix (30/40/15/7.5/7.5).
- `BigCycle binary`: original discrete regime classifier — hard thresholds
  on yield curve, inflation, and real rate pick one of four regimes and
  apply the full corresponding nudge from `REGIME_NUDGES`.
- `BigCycle scored`: consumes `src.indicators.regime_classifier()` to
  produce a continuous score in `[0, 1]` per regime, then blends the
  per-regime nudges weighted by those scores.
